Petition To Determine Validity Of Second Amendment To Trust; Petition For Instructions Regarding Attorney Fees; Petition To Suspend Trust And Etc.
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Continued To Apr-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Shelton for and with Carl Reece. Ciarin O'Sullivan and Randall Schmidt for Lydia Barta (Petitioner).

Mr. Shelton reported that mediation is set for April 9, 2015 with Bette Epstein. The Petition to Determine Validity of Second Amendment to Trust, etc. and the Petition to Transfer to Los Angeles County were continued to April 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the parties have not resolved the matter by then, they are to meet and confer as to how proceed. The Court will not rule on the issue raised by Mr. O'Sullivan regarding Ms. Barta's prayer with respect to barring the Trustee from using Trust funds to defend the restatement. The Parties agreed to continue with the stipulation regarding the issue of Ms. Barta continuing to enjoy use of the trust property. The March 10, 2015 hearing date on status of mediation was vacated.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Saephan. (Not Reported)

PETITION TO TRANSFER TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Continued To Apr-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Second Amendment To Trust; Petition For Instructions Regarding Attorney Fees; Petition To Suspend Trust And Etc.
Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Continued To Jan-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan and Randy Schmidt for and with Lydia Barta. Peter Shelton for Carl Reece.

Mr. Shelton stated that his Client will be objecting and would like to file a petition for change of venue. Mr. O'Sullivan and Mr. Schmidt indicated that they will contest venue, asked the Court to suspend the Trustee in light of his financial circumstances, and argued that someone else will be the Trustee and that all of the property and beneficiaries are located here. The petition to determine validity of second amendment to Trust was continued to January 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a petition for change of venue (to be given the same hearing date). The stipulation pursuant to which the parties agreed to limiting authorization of the Trustee pending the Court's resolution of the current petition will continue. The Parties agreed to an informal accounting and were encouraged by the Court to share information. The Parties also agreed to participate in mediation without prejudice to the Court determing the issue of venue. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 27, 2015. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 27, 2015. The Parties are to return on March 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
